pairs.label: Help
differenceNotes: Wunderland has 17+ commands vs OpenClaw ~12
Wunderland adds: seal, list-presets, skills, models, plugins, export, import

pairs.label: Version
differenceNotes: Different versioning: OpenClaw uses calendar (2026.x.x), Wunderland uses semver (0.x.x)

pairs.label: Doctor-Health-Check
differenceNotes: Both check system health
Wunderland checks HEXACO personality config, security tier, and extension health

pairs.label: Status
differenceNotes: Wunderland shows security tier, personality preset, active channels

pairs.label: Models
differenceNotes: Wunderland supports 13 LLM providers vs OpenClaw ~4
Wunderland includes SmallModelResolver for cost optimization

pairs.label: Skills
differenceNotes: Wunderland has 18 curated skills with SKILL.md descriptors
OpenClaw uses community skill repository

pairs.label: Agent Presets
differenceNotes: Wunderland-only: 8 agent presets (research, support, creative, code-review, data, security, devops, personal)
Each preset includes HEXACO traits, security tier, skill suggestions, persona document

pairs.label: Channels
differenceNotes: Wunderland: 29 channel platforms
OpenClaw: ~8 channel platforms
Wunderland adds: Signal, iMessage, Zalo, Nostr, Twitch, Line, Feishu, Mattermost, NextcloudTalk, Tlon

pairs.label: Security Acknowledgment
differenceNotes: OpenClaw-only: requires security acknowledgment before setup begins

pairs.label: Setup Mode Selection
differenceNotes: Both offer QuickStart vs Advanced; OpenClaw calls it "Onboarding mode"

pairs.label: Agent / Workspace Name
differenceNotes: OpenClaw: "workspace directory", Wunderland: "agent name"

pairs.label: Observability Config
differenceNotes: Wunderland-only: OpenTelemetry configuration (off/traces+metrics/full)

pairs.label: LLM Provider
differenceNotes: OpenClaw: ~30 providers (model/auth provider), Wunderland: 13 providers multi-select

pairs.label: Auth Method
differenceNotes: OpenClaw-only: OAuth (Codex) vs API key auth; Wunderland uses direct API key only

pairs.label: API Key Provision
differenceNotes: OpenClaw-only: choose how to provide API key (paste, env var, etc.)

pairs.label: Use Existing Key
differenceNotes: OpenClaw auto-detects OPENAI_API_KEY from environment

pairs.label: API Key Input
differenceNotes: Both require API key; Wunderland also offers .env paste import

pairs.label: Model Selection
differenceNotes: Similar model selection; Wunderland includes SmallModelResolver for cost optimization

pairs.label: Channel Selection
differenceNotes: OpenClaw: ~20 channels, Wunderland: ~30 platforms with emoji icons

pairs.label: Daemon / Background Mode
differenceNotes: OpenClaw-only: built-in daemon mode (LaunchAgent/systemd)

pairs.label: Skills Selection
differenceNotes: Both have skills; Wunderland skills appear in Advanced mode with paginated catalog

pairs.label: Review & Confirm
differenceNotes: Both show review/summary before finalizing
